executive_summary: AFSA is a small, expertise-heavy Treasury-portfolio regulator responsible for Australia's personal insolvency and personal property securities (PPSR) systems, plus criminal-asset management. Its 2025-26 agenda is dominated by a multi-year data and technology transformation (replacing legacy systems, building analytics/intelligence-led regulation and cyber resilience), workforce capability uplift, and a shift to a more visible, intelligence-led 'regulatory steward'. An ANAO performance audit into AFSA's management of conflicts of interest (Report No.4 2024-25) is a live governance/probity signal. Critically for BD, AFSA is explicitly cutting contractor/consultant use (over 40% in 2024-25, a further 1% target in 2025-26) under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework, so opportunities must be specialist, time-bound and clearly non-core, with knowledge transfer built in.

strategic_priorities.title: Digital and data transformation
strategic_priorities.description: Replace outdated core systems with modern, scalable platforms and become a more effective data-driven regulator, delivering a Technology Strategy and building data/analytics capability.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We are continuing our data and technology transformation to replace outdated systems with modern platforms... build the strong foundations for AFSA to be a more effective data-driven regulator.

strategic_priorities.title: Intelligence-led regulatory stewardship
strategic_priorities.description: Strengthen and target the regulatory posture using data, insights and intelligence, including an annual Regulatory Action Statement and a PPSR misuse detection pilot.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We adopt a whole-of-system approach to regulation, using data and intelligence to prioritise where we focus our resources.

strategic_priorities.title: Workforce capability and culture
strategic_priorities.description: Deliver the Workforce Strategy 2023-28: build regulatory expertise, data literacy, leadership capability and a learning culture, while rebuilding employee engagement after a census dip.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: AFSA's Workforce Strategy identifies 3 key priorities: Capability, Culture, Capacity.

strategic_priorities.title: Cyber security resilience
strategic_priorities.description: Continually improve protection of systems, data and people as digital delivery and cyber risks rise.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Strengthen cyber security... continually improve how we protect our systems, data, and people.

strategic_priorities.title: Vulnerability and access
strategic_priorities.description: Deliver targeted initiatives under the Vulnerability Strategy 2025-28 and improve clarity, reach and timeliness of information for under-represented groups.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We will deliver targeted initiatives aligned to key objectives of the Vulnerability Strategy 2025-28.

reform_and_change_drivers: Partnership with Treasury on reform proposals to keep the personal insolvency and PPSR systems fair and balanced
Ministerial Statement of Expectations and Statement of Intent shaping key activities
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework driving reduced reliance on contractors/consultants and APS-as-default for core work
Economic pressure (rising unemployment, modest growth, elevated credit risk) lifting personal insolvency volumes
Emerging technology and digital assets (AI, cryptocurrency, NFTs) reshaping the credit system and creating new regulatory and intelligence demands
Legacy system replacement and an enterprise-wide data/technology uplift
ANAO performance audit into management of conflicts of interest
challenges_and_risks: Funding sustainability named as an enterprise risk for a small agency with finite own-resourcing
Strategy and transformation risk - delivering the multi-year tech/data transformation
Employee engagement dipped (census index fell from 73 to 69 in 2024) requiring culture rebuild
Several performance measures are new or baseline-only (RAP1 baseline, ROE2 under development), signalling an immature outcomes-measurement framework for a regulator
Reliance on external platform managers (e.g. Fujitsu for PPSR) for critical register availability
Balancing regulating vulnerability against regulating misuse across competing stakeholder interests

news_and_controversies.summary: AFSA's Inspector-General in Bankruptcy took public enforcement action against a registered trustee (Gavin King, linked to the Paul Leroy matter), and separately put insolvency practitioners 'on notice' over system misuse (May 2025).
news_and_controversies.implication: Confirms an active, public enforcement and integrity posture - latent demand for practitioner-oversight, investigations support and data-driven risk-targeting capability.

news_and_controversies.summary: AFSA published its regulatory focus for 2025-26 addressing four key harms.
news_and_controversies.implication: Provides the published priority 'harms' to align any analytics, compliance-framework or surveillance offering against.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO conducted a dedicated performance audit, 'Management of Conflicts of Interest by the Australian Financial Security Authority' (Auditor-General Report No.4, 2024-25). The audit's specific conclusions and recommendations were not verified in the supplied research.
anao_findings.implication: A COI-focused audit is a strong, specific governance/probity demand signal - likely follow-on need for COI framework and declaration-process uplift, probity advisory and independent assurance against any recommendations.

opportunity_areas.area: Core system modernisation / Technology Strategy delivery
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Replace outdated core business systems with modern, scalable platforms that support contemporary regulatory practice and a better client experience.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and service design for legacy replacement, with business/process analysis and staged solution implementation/integration - scoped as a specialist, knowledge-transfer engagement complementing AFSA's APS delivery team.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly commits to a data and technology transformation 'to replace outdated systems with modern platforms' and to deliver a Technology Strategy across 2025-29; the firm can build and implement, not just advise.

opportunity_areas.area: Data and analytics capability for intelligence-led regulation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Become a more effective data-driven regulator: invest in data/analytics capabilities, surveillance and detection tools, and a PPSR misuse pilot.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Build an analytics/risk-targeting capability - data management, business intelligence and a misuse-detection model supporting the ROE2 PPSR pilot and the annual Regulatory Action Statement.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to 'invest in data and analytics capabilities' and to a 2025-26 pilot to 'systematically detect and assess compliance of registrations'; AFSA's enforcement posture (practitioner 'on notice') reinforces demand.

opportunity_areas.area: Conflicts of interest / governance and probity uplift
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Respond to ANAO scrutiny of conflicts-of-interest management and strengthen governance and accountability arrangements.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A COI and governance framework uplift - declaration processes, registers and committee/accountability arrangements - with independent assurance against any ANAO recommendations (secondary: Audit & Assurance; Procurement, Probity & Contracts for probity).
opportunity_areas.rationale: ANAO Report No.4 2024-25 specifically audited AFSA's management of conflicts of interest; the plan also lists 'establish robust governance and accountability arrangements' as an action.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Strengthen protection of systems, data and people against rising cyber and digital-delivery risks.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A security assessment and assurance of core registers and case-management systems, plus cyber advisory aligned to the Technology Strategy.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names 'Strengthen cyber security' as a capability action and identifies 'increasing cybersecurity and digital delivery risks' in the CEO's foreword.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Several measures are new, baseline-only or 'under development' (ROE2, RAP1), reflecting an immature outcomes framework for a regulator that admits measuring public value is hard.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A PGPA-aligned performance-measure redesign for a regulator - converting output/baseline measures into robust effectiveness measures and benchmarks.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan notes 'measuring performance is complex', sets baselines for RAP1 and flags ROE2 as developing toward a quantitative measure once benchmarks exist.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Funding sustainability is a named enterprise risk for a small agency reliant on appropriations and register-based activity.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability review or activity-based costing model linking regulatory/registry/criminal-asset activity to cost, supporting funding decisions.
opportunity_areas.rationale: 'Funding sustainability' is explicitly listed among AFSA's enterprise risks, and the criminal-assets efficiency measure shows an existing cost-to-value focus.

recommended_actions.action: Request a meeting with AFSA's CIO/technology leadership to discuss the Technology Strategy and core system replacement, leading with a specialist, knowledge-transfer delivery model that respects the Strategic Commissioning Framework.
recommended_actions.rationale: System modernisation is the largest multi-year program in the plan, but AFSA is cutting consultants - the winning angle is specialist capability with built-in transfer to APS staff, not bums-on-seats.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short capability note on COI/governance framework uplift and independent assurance, timed to AFSA's response to ANAO Report No.4 2024-25, and seek a conversation with the Chief Risk Officer / audit and risk committee.
recommended_actions.rationale: An ANAO COI audit creates time-bound, defensible demand for governance and probity work that is corporate/enabling and less politically sensitive.

recommended_actions.action: Pull the ANAO audit's conclusions and recommendations verbatim before engaging, and watch aph.gov.au (Economics Legislation Committee Estimates) for AFSA appearances.
recommended_actions.rationale: The research confirmed the audit exists but not its findings; grounding the pitch in actual recommendations is essential and Estimates may reveal further pressure.

recommended_actions.action: Develop an analytics/misuse-detection proposition aligned to AFSA's published four key harms and the ROE2 PPSR pilot, targeting the regulatory/intelligence function.
recommended_actions.rationale: AFSA's intelligence-led posture and active enforcement create demand for risk-targeting analytics, but the offer must map to its named harms to be credible.

recommended_actions.action: Qualify the consultant-reduction constraint early in any approach and frame all offerings as specialist, time-bound and transfer-focused.
recommended_actions.rationale: AFSA has cut contractors by 40%+ and defaults to APS for core work; ignoring this will sink otherwise-good proposals.

engagement_targets: Chief Executive / Inspector-General in Bankruptcy (Tim Beresford) - for strategic transformation framing
Chief Information / Digital Officer or technology delivery lead - Technology Strategy and core system replacement
Chief Risk Officer and the Audit and Risk Committee - COI/governance response to ANAO
Chief Financial Officer / corporate enabling group - funding sustainability and costing
Head of regulatory operations / intelligence function - analytics and misuse-detection
Head of People / Workforce (Workforce Strategy 2023-28) - capability uplift (note consultant-reduction constraint)
talking_points: Your Technology Strategy commits to replacing outdated core systems - we can build and implement, not just advise, with a specialist team that transfers capability to your APS staff in line with the Strategic Commissioning Framework.
The ANAO conflicts-of-interest audit is an opportunity to get ahead: a tight governance/COI framework uplift plus independent assurance against the recommendations.
You've set out four key harms and an intelligence-led posture - we can stand up the analytics and misuse-detection capability (including the ROE2 PPSR pilot) to make that operational.
Funding sustainability is a named enterprise risk - an activity-based costing view of regulatory, registry and criminal-asset work can strengthen your funding case.
Several of your new measures are baseline-only - a PGPA-aligned redesign would give you defensible effectiveness measures for a regulator where public value is hard to show.
We understand you're reducing consultant reliance - everything we propose is specialist, time-bound and built around knowledge transfer.
caveats: This is a clear, well-structured plan but for a small regulator that is actively and explicitly reducing contractor/consultant spend (40%+ in 2024-25, default to APS for core work), which materially dampens BD prospects and caps engagement size - opportunities must be genuinely specialist, time-bound and transfer-focused. Most opportunities are inferred from capability/transformation language rather than named procurements, hence Medium/Low confidence. The external research was incomplete: the ANAO COI audit was confirmed but its specific findings/recommendations were not verified, and no parliamentary material was confirmed (a gap, not a confirmed absence). Some enforcement news reflects AFSA acting as regulator rather than controversies about AFSA itself.
